explanation:
$$\text {} \begin{aligned} & I=\int_0^\pi\left[\cos ^2\left(\frac{3 \pi}{8}-\frac{x}{4}\right)-\cos ^2\left(\frac{11 \pi}{8}+\frac{x}{4}\right)\right] d x \\ &= \int_0^\pi\left[\cos ^2\left(\frac{3 \pi}{8}-\frac{x}{4}\right)-\cos ^2\left(\pi+\left(\frac{3 \pi}{8}+\frac{x}{4}\right)\right] d x\right. \\ &=\int_0^\pi\left[\cos ^2\left(\frac{3 \pi}{8}-\frac{x}{4}\right)-\cos ^2\left(\frac{3 \pi}{8}+\frac{x}{4}\right)\right] d x \end{aligned}$$

$=\int_0^\pi\left[\begin{array}{r}\cos \left(\frac{3 \pi}{8}-\frac{x}{4}+\frac{3 \pi}{8}+\frac{x}{4}\right) \\ \cdot \cos \left(\frac{3 \pi}{8}-\frac{x}{4}-\frac{3 \pi}{8}-\frac{x}{4}\right)\end{array}\right]$

$$\begin{aligned} & \begin{array}{l} =\int_0^\pi \cos \left(\frac{3 \pi}{4}\right) \cdot \cos \left(-\frac{x}{2}\right) \cdot d x \\ =-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int_0^\pi \cos \left(\frac{-x}{2}\right) d x \\ \text { Let }-\frac{x}{2} =t \Rightarrow d x=-2 d t \end{array}\\ & =-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}-2 \cos t \cdot d t \\ & =\frac{2}{\sqrt{2}} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos t \cdot d t \\ & \\ & =\sqrt{2}[\sin t]_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}=\sqrt{2} \end{aligned}$$

content: The value of $$\int_\lambda^{\lambda+\pi / 2}\left(\cos ^4 x+\sin ^4 x\right) d x$$ is
explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} & \text {Let } I=\int_\lambda^{\lambda+\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\cos ^4 x+\sin ^4 x\right) \text { is } \frac{\pi}{2} \\ & \therefore \quad I=\int_\lambda^{\lambda+\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\cos ^4 x+\sin ^4 x\right) d x \\ & =\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\cos ^4 x+\sin ^4 x\right) d x \\ \end{aligned}$$

$$\left[\because\right.$$ If $$T$$ is period of $$f(x)$$, then $$\int_a^{a+T} f(x)$$ is independent of $$a$$ ]

$$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow \quad 1 & =\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos ^4 x d x+\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sin ^4 x d x \\ \Rightarrow \quad I & =\left(\frac{3}{4} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{\pi}{2}\right)+\left(\frac{3}{4} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\ & =2 \times \frac{3}{4} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{\pi}{2}=\frac{3 \pi}{8} \end{aligned}$$

content: $$\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{(n+1)(n+2) \ldots 3 n}{n^{2 n}}\right)^{1 / n}$$ is equal to
explanation:
Let $$L=\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{(n+1) \cdot(n+2) \ldots(3 n)}{n^{2 n}}\right)^{1 / n}$$

$$=\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\left(\frac{n+1}{n}\right)\left(\frac{n+2}{n}\right) \ldots\left(\frac{n+2 n}{n}\right)\right)^{1 / n} $$

On taking log both sides,

$$\begin{aligned} \log L & =\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n}\left[\log \left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)+\log \left(1+\frac{2}{n}\right)\right. \\ & \left.\left.+\ldots \log \left(1+\frac{2 n}{n}\right)\right]\right] \\ & =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{r=1}^{2 n} \log \left(1+\frac{r}{n}\right) \\ & =\left[[\log (1+x) \cdot x]_0^2-\int \frac{1}{1+x} \cdot x \cdot d x\right]_0^2 \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \log L=[\log (1+x) \cdot x]_0^2-\int_0^{2 x+1-1} \frac{1+x}{1+x} \\ & =2 \log (3)-[x-\log (1+x)]_0^2 \\ & =2 \log (3)-[2-\log 3] \\ & =3 \log (3)-2=\log (27)-2 \\ L & =e^{\log (27)-2}=27 e^{-2}=27 / e^2 \end{aligned}$$

content: If $$X \phi(x)=\int_\limits5^x 3 t^2-2 \phi(t) d t, x>-2$$ and $$\phi(0)=4$$, then $$\phi(2)$$ is
explanation:
$$\begin{array}{ll} \text { Given, } & X \phi(x)=\int_5^x 3 t^2-2 \phi^{\prime}(t) d t \\ \Rightarrow & X \phi(x)=\left[t^3-2 \phi(t)\right]_5^x \\ \Rightarrow & X \phi(x)=\left[x^3-125\right]-2[\phi(x)-\phi(5)] \end{array}$$

Now, $$\phi(0)=4$$

$$\begin{array}{ll} \Rightarrow & 0=-125-2[4-\phi(5)] \\ \Rightarrow & \phi(5)=\frac{133}{2} \end{array}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \begin{array}{l} \text { For } \phi(2), \\ \Rightarrow 2 \phi(2)=(8-125)-2\left[\phi(2)-\frac{133}{2}\right] \end{array} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 4 \phi(2)=16 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \phi(2)=4 \\ \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{array}{ll} \text { Let } & I=\int \frac{d x}{1+\tan x} \\ \Rightarrow & I=\int \frac{\cos x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x \quad \text{.... (i)} \end{array}$$

and

$$\begin{aligned} & I=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)+\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)} d x \\ & \Rightarrow \quad I=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sin x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x \quad \text{.... (ii)} \end{aligned}$$

$$\left\{\because \int_0^a f(x) d x=\int_0^a f(a-x) d x\right\}$$

On adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$\begin{aligned} 2I & =\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x=\int_0^{\pi / 2} 1 d x=[x]^{\pi / 2}_0 \\ \Rightarrow \quad 2I & =\frac{\pi}{2} \\ \Rightarrow \quad I & =\frac{\pi}{4} \end{aligned}$$
